findings.message: Agent references a non-existent skill path; skill context will be absent
Testing agent has no examples
Planning agent has no examples
No tools field declared; agent will silently have no tools or inherit all from parent, neither of which is the intended behavior
No tools field; the agent's core function (running artifact_query.py) requires Bash which is not declared
Agent references a non-existent skill path; skill will silently not load
Agent runs shell commands in its instructions but Bash is not in the declared tools list
Primary implementation agent has no examples
Agent shows bash-dependent workflow without Bash declared in tools
Maestro's agent registry is stale; references three deleted agents that no longer exist
Maestro orchestrator has no examples despite being the primary workflow coordinator
Agent has no tools field; least-privilege principle violated
Agent references a non-existent skill path
Agent has a verification workflow that requires Bash but it is not declared in tools
Agent references three non-existent sub-agents by name; Task spawns will fail at runtime
Agent has no tools field
Scout has no examples; developers new to the project cannot see typical invocations
Agent references a non-existent skill path
Bug investigation agent has no examples
Agent's rp-cli workflow requires Bash but it is not declared in tools
validate-agent uses model: haiku in violation of the project's no-haiku rule
Agent has no tools field; in addition to the model violation, this agent lacks tool constraints
Non-standard frontmatter field name (underscore vs hyphen); parsers checking for `user-invocable` will silently miss this skill's invocability declaration
Skill's declared name (planning-agent) does not match its folder path (plan-agent); causes confusion in skill activation and cross-references
Skill instructs Claude to pipe a remote shell script directly to sh with no integrity check
subprocess.run with shell=True and user-controlled string argument — shell injection risk if workspace name contains metacharacters
Setup script prints curl-pipe-to-sh install instruction for Lean elan; not auto-executed but propagates insecure install pattern to users
Setup wizard prints curl-pipe-to-sh install instruction for Lean elan at lines 229, 560, and 1136

Skill instructs Claude to pipe a remote shell script directly to sh with no integrity check — Line 22: `curl https://raw.githubusercontent.com/leanprover/elan/master/elan-init.sh -sSf | sh` — live shell-pipe in a skill file loaded into Claude context as instructions

subprocess.run with shell=True and user-controlled string argument — shell injection risk if workspace name contains metacharacters — Line 47: `subprocess.run(cmd, shell=True, ...)` where cmd is an f-string built from user-provided workspace/task arguments passed via CLI args
Setup script prints curl-pipe-to-sh install instruction for Lean elan; not auto-executed but propagates insecure install pattern to users
Setup wizard prints curl-pipe-to-sh install instruction for Lean elan at lines 229, 560, and 1136

Agent references three non-existent sub-agents by name; Task spawns will fail at runtime — Agents `codebase-locator`, `codebase-analyzer`, `codebase-pattern-finder` referenced in prompt body — none exist in .claude/agents/
Maestro's agent registry is stale; references three deleted agents that no longer exist — Agent Reference table in maestro.md lists `turbo`, `pioneer`, `nexus` — none exist in .claude/agents/
Agent references a non-existent skill path; skill will silently not load — Prompt instructs loading `.claude/skills/debug/SKILL.md` — path does not exist; no `debug/` skill folder found
Agent references a non-existent skill path — Prompt references `.claude/skills/create_plan/SKILL.md` — no `create_plan/` folder exists in .claude/skills/
Agent references a non-existent skill path; skill context will be absent — Prompt references `.claude/skills/agentica-sdk/SKILL.md` — no `agentica-sdk/` skill folder exists
Agent references a non-existent skill path — Prompt references `.claude/skills/braintrust-analyze/SKILL.md` — no such skill exists; `braintrust-tracing/SKILL.md` is the closest match

No tools field declared; agent will silently have no tools or inherit all from parent, neither of which is the intended behavior — Agent has no `tools:` frontmatter field but prompt body runs Python scripts via Bash and writes output files

No tools field; the agent's core function (running artifact_query.py) requires Bash which is not declared — Agent has no `tools:` field but prompt runs `artifact_query.py` via Bash; Bash is unavailable without declaration

Agent runs shell commands in its instructions but Bash is not in the declared tools list — Tools declares [Read, Grep, Glob] but prompt shows `git diff HEAD` and `uv run pytest` commands that require Bash
Agent's rp-cli workflow requires Bash but it is not declared in tools — Tools declares [Read, Grep, Glob] but prompt shows `rp-cli snapshot` and `rp-cli switch` commands requiring Bash
Agent shows bash-dependent workflow without Bash declared in tools — Tools declares [Read, Grep, Glob] but prompt shows `cat FILE` and `rp-cli` bash commands
Agent has a verification workflow that requires Bash but it is not declared in tools — Tools declares [Read, Grep, Glob] but 'Verification Commands' section shows bash commands that require Bash

Skill's declared name (planning-agent) does not match its folder path (plan-agent); causes confusion in skill activation and cross-references — Frontmatter line 2: `name: planning-agent` but file is at `.claude/skills/plan-agent/SKILL.md`; skill activation by folder name `plan-agent` will find a skill declaring a different name

Non-standard frontmatter field name (underscore vs hyphen); parsers checking for `user-invocable` will silently miss this skill's invocability declaration — Frontmatter line 4: `user_invocable: true` — canonical field name uses hyphen: `user-invocable`

validate-agent uses model: haiku in violation of the project's no-haiku rule — Frontmatter: `model: haiku` — project rule `.claude/rules/no-haiku.md` explicitly states: 'Never use model: haiku when spawning agents via the Task tool'
Agent has no tools field; least-privilege principle violated — No `tools:` field present in frontmatter; agent inherits all parent tools
Agent has no tools field — No `tools:` field in frontmatter
Agent has no tools field; in addition to the model violation, this agent lacks tool constraints — No `tools:` field in frontmatter
Maestro orchestrator has no examples despite being the primary workflow coordinator — No `## Examples` section found in agent body
Scout has no examples; developers new to the project cannot see typical invocations — No `## Examples` section found in agent body
Primary implementation agent has no examples — No `## Examples` section found in agent body
Testing agent has no examples — No `## Examples` section found in agent body
Bug investigation agent has no examples — No `## Examples` section found in agent body
Planning agent has no examples — No `## Examples` section found in agent body
